brands.name: Leadership Management International
brands.notes: Waco, TX-based leadership and management development franchise (founded 1966) selling coaching/training programs to corporate clients. One of the lowest-cost franchises in the Franchise 500: home-based, no ad fund, with franchisor financing of most of the fee. Headline unit count (495) is global across 80+ countries; the US footprint is closer to 110 locations.

riskFlags.title: Headline 495 units are mostly international; ~110 in the US
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's directory lists 495 units, but LMI operates in 80+ countries and Vetted Biz counts roughly 110 US locations - so domestic brand presence and peer network are far thinner than the headline count suggests.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz - LMI Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation
riskFlags.detail: Public FDD summaries do not show earnings claims for LMI franchisees; buyers must validate income potential entirely through franchisee calls. Revenue depends on the individual's B2B sales ability - the franchise is effectively a personal consulting/sales practice.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - LMI Franchise Insights; Franchise Direct FDD summary

riskFlags.title: Short 5-year franchise term
riskFlags.detail: The initial franchise agreement runs five years, renewable for consecutive five-year periods unless terminated for violations - a shorter commitment than typical 10-year franchise terms, cutting both ways (flexibility vs. renewal risk).
riskFlags.source: Franchise Direct FDD summary; Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: No litigation or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report LMI does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its FDD - a clean Item 3/Item 4 record for a 60-year-old system.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - LMI Franchise Insights

nationwide.note: Entrepreneur lists LMI as seeking new franchisees throughout the US and internationally (80+ countries); home-based territory model with no published state exclusions.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Direct FDD summary

totalInvestmentMax.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Direct FDD summary

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Direct FDD summary

adFundPercent.note: No national ad fund fee listed.

totalUnits.note: Global count across 80+ countries; Vetted Biz counts ~110 US locations.

yearFranchising.note: Vetted Biz cites 1995 for US franchising start.

trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at Waco HQ per Vetted Biz; Entrepreneur lists 40 classroom hours.

renewalTermYears.note: 5-year initial term, renewable in consecutive 5-year periods.

financingOffered.note: Franchisor finances up to $10,000 of the fee at 0% interest, monthly payments up to 36 months.

veteranDiscount.note: 80% interest-free financing on the franchise fee for veterans.

businessModelType.source: Franchise Direct (programs sold to individuals and corporate clients)

internationalAvailability.source: Franchise Local / LMI corporate (80+ countries, 25+ languages)

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Direct / Vetted Biz FDD summaries (2025 FDD)

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz / public FDD summaries (no earnings claim shown)

brands.name: Great American Cookies
brands.notes: Mall-based cookie-cake and fresh-baked cookie franchise founded 1977 in Atlanta, ~350-395 US units, owned by FAT Brands. The brand itself is durable, but the parent-company situation is the story: FAT Brands filed Chapter 11 in January 2026 with $1.3B of debt, and Great American Cookies franchisees are among those pressing claims over supply-cost increases and ad-fund use. FAT Brands does not publish a state-by-state availability list for the brand, so state availability is omitted here.

riskFlags.title: Parent FAT Brands filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy (January 2026) with $1.3B debt
riskFlags.detail: FAT Brands, Great American Cookies' multi-brand parent, filed for Chapter 11 protection in January 2026 carrying roughly $1.3 billion in debt - creating uncertainty over brand investment, supply chain, and franchise support while the restructuring plays out.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive; Fox Business

riskFlags.title: Franchisees claim cookie-batter overcharges (+88% since 2021) and misused ad funds in bankruptcy court
riskFlags.detail: In the FAT Brands bankruptcy, Great American Cookies franchisees allege required chocolate-chip batter prices rose 8% (2022), 20% (2023), and 44% (2024) - up 88% since July 2021 - and franchisees across FAT brands claim tens of millions in damages from misused ad funds and rebates diverted to the parent.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Business Online - Franchisees push their claims in the Fat Brands bankruptcy

riskFlags.title: Shrinking unit count and mall-traffic dependence
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data showed 354 franchised units in 2023 with a roughly -4% year-over-year decline; the concept is concentrated in shopping malls, tying unit economics to mall foot traffic.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback Great American Cookies FDD data

riskFlags.title: Former parent chairman's federal charges were dropped in 2025
riskFlags.detail: The DOJ moved to dismiss its indictment of FAT Brands chairman Andy Wiederhorn (accused of concealing $47M in payments from the company), removing one overhang - but the episode preceded and fed into the company's broader financial distress.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Business Online

liquidCapitalMin.note: Vetted Biz cites $75,000 minimum; other directories cite $100,000 liquid / $250,000 net worth.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights (2025 FDD-derived)
totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS cites a wider $110,000-$512,000 range covering smaller formats/conversions.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights (2025 FDD-derived)

adFundPercent.note: FranchisePayback cites 4%; Vetted Biz cites 2% - confirm in current FDD.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights (FDD-derived)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales estimate; franchisor does not publish an official Item 19 AUV. FranchisePayback estimates $525,000.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights
totalUnits.note: US locations; FranchisePayback counted 354 franchised units in 2023 with -4% YoY trend.

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Great American Cookies Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at the Atlanta corporate training facility.

bankruptcyDisclosed.source: Restaurant Dive / Restaurant Business (parent FAT Brands Chapter 11, Jan 2026); Vetted Biz notes lawsuits/bankruptcy disclosed in FDD

businessModelType.source: Great American Cookies brand site (mall retail bakery)

mobileBased.source: Great American Cookies brand site (mall/in-line retail model)

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz / FranchisePayback FDD analyses (2025 FDD)

item19Available.source: SHARPSHEETS / FranchisePayback (franchisor does not officially disclose AUV)

brands.name: Golden Corral
brands.notes: America's largest grill-buffet chain (Raleigh, NC; founded 1973, franchising since 1987), ~351 US restaurants of which all but 3 are franchised. A capital-intensive, large-format play in the post-COVID buffet segment: strong AUVs at top units but multi-million-dollar builds, franchisee bankruptcies, and store closures in weaker markets. The franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted here.

riskFlags.title: Franchisee bankruptcies and closures through 2025-2026
riskFlags.detail: Golden Corral's largest franchise operator previously filed Chapter 11, and in June 2025 franchisee South Texas Corral LLC filed Chapter 11 listing $150K assets against $1.64M liabilities; franchisees continued closing locations into early 2026 (e.g., Canton, OH).
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Business Online; TheStreet closure coverage

riskFlags.title: Very long estimated payback on a $3.6M-$8.7M build
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz's FDD-derived analysis estimates a 17.7-19.7 year investment payback at the system's $4.2M average gross sales - the large-format buffet build (up to $8.7M including real estate) is among the most capital-intensive in family dining.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Golden Corral Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Aggressive enforcement: franchisor won $1M+ from franchisees who abandoned a restaurant
riskFlags.detail: A New Jersey federal court awarded Golden Corral over $1 million in consequential damages from franchisees who abandoned their Vineland, NJ restaurant; separately, a Poughkeepsie, NY franchisee sued alleging her license was terminated after 52 days over minor infractions, claiming ~$10M in losses.
riskFlags.source: Foley & Lardner case note; Spectacular Magazine

riskFlags.title: Buffet segment concentration risk
riskFlags.detail: The brand is a pure-play all-you-can-eat buffet operator - a segment hit hard by COVID and labor/food inflation; published top-quartile AUV ($6.4M for 11-month 2024 restaurants) sits well above the $4.2M system average, so site selection drives outcomes.
riskFlags.source: Golden Corral franchise qualifications page (franchisor); Vetted Biz

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Golden Corral Franchise Insights (minimum cash required)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Golden Corral 2025 FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor site frames the range as $2.7M-$7M depending on restaurant size and market; FDD Item 7 range $3,557,604-$6,090,626 (to $8.7M including real estate per Vetted Biz).

totalInvestmentMax.note: Up to $8,738,837 including real-estate purchase per Vetted Biz.

adFundPercent.source: Golden Corral 2025 FDD (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Golden Corral 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Vetted Biz)
avgUnitRevenue.note: System yearly gross sales; franchisor cites $6.4M 2024 AUV for top-performing 11-month restaurants.

totalUnits.source: Golden Corral 2025 FDD outlet data (via Vetted Biz/SHARPSHEETS)

yearFounded.source: Vetted Biz - Golden Corral Franchise Insights

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Golden Corral Franchise Insights

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Golden Corral Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Initial training at Raleigh HQ; restaurant management experience expected of operators.

mobileBased.source: Golden Corral brand site (large-format buffet restaurants)

isGrowing.source: Restaurant Business / TheStreet closure coverage; Wikipedia unit history
isGrowing.note: System has contracted from its ~490-unit pre-COVID peak; franchisee closures continued into 2026 even as a smaller-format 'Homestead Kitchen' concept tests.

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS / Franchise Direct 2025 FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz (Item 19-derived AUV); franchisor publishes top-quartile AUV

brands.name: Destination Athlete
brands.notes: Home-based 'athletic team dealer' franchise (founded/franchising 2008): franchisees sell equipment, apparel, fundraising solutions, and performance services to youth, high-school, and college teams in an exclusive territory. Very low entry cost and fast unit growth, but revenue is a relationship-sales grind with enormous per-unit dispersion.

riskFlags.title: Huge revenue dispersion: units range from $15K to $488K per year
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived Item 19 summaries show average annual revenue of $346,313 but a floor of just $15,000 and a top of $487,706 - outcomes depend almost entirely on the individual operator's sales relationships with local schools and teams.
riskFlags.source: Franchimp / franchise-profile Item 19 summaries (FDD-derived)

riskFlags.title: Variable fees: franchise fee runs $20K-$50K and royalty 5%-9%
riskFlags.detail: Both the initial fee and the ongoing royalty are disclosed as ranges (fee $20,000-$50,000; royalty 5%-9% plus 2% ad fund per Entrepreneur), so effective economics differ materially by territory/agreement - get the exact schedule for your deal in writing.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: Young, fast-scaling system (+35% units)
riskFlags.detail: Units grew 73 (2019) to 128 (2020) to 151 (2021) and now ~280 with a +35.3% trend per Entrepreneur - rapid recruiting of low-cost home-based territories means many peers are inexperienced and system support is scaling in real time.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; FDD-derived unit history via franchise directories

nationwide.note: Exclusive territories advertised in all 50 states plus DC; franchisor site carries standard registration-state disclaimers (CA, FL, HI, IL, IN, MD, MI, MN, NY, ND, OR, RI, SD, VA, WA, WI).

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise directory FDD summaries (Why Franchise/IFPG cite 7%; Entrepreneur lists 5%-9%)
royaltyPercent.note: Royalty disclosed as a 5%-9% range; 7% of gross sales most commonly cited.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average annual revenue per unit; range $15,000-$487,706. Verify against the current FDD Item 19.

totalUnits.note: All franchised; directories cited 229 in the prior year.

trainingWeeks.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (80+ on-the-job hours plus 40+ classroom hours)

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing relationships available per Entrepreneur directory

businessModelType.source: Destination Athlete franchising page (sells to youth/HS/college teams and organizations)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Direct relationship selling to local schools, athletic teams, and organizations (group sales channels)

seasonalityNotes.value: Demand tracks the school-year sports calendar (team equipment/apparel/fundraising cycles by season)
seasonalityNotes.source: Destination Athlete franchising page (team dealer model serving school sports seasons)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2022 FDD); SHARPSHEETS 2025 analysis

brands.name: Caring Transitions
brands.notes: Largest US senior relocation, downsizing, and estate-sale franchise (Cincinnati-based C.T. Franchising Systems, part of the Strategic Franchising Systems family; founded/franchising 2006). Home-based, low-overhead model riding demographic tailwinds, with strong unit growth (+58 in 2024 to 372 territories). Headline revenues are modest: median unit grossed ~$203K in 2024, so it's an owner-operator income business, not a passive one.

riskFlags.title: Median unit grossed just $202,811 in 2024
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD Item 19: across 253 franchises open the full year, average gross receipts were $284,485 but the median was $202,811 (63% average gross profit before owner labor, marketing minimums, and royalties) - real take-home depends on the owner running lean.
riskFlags.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Stacked minimum fees bite at low revenue
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 6% of gross receipts with a $500/month minimum after year one, plus a 2% national branding fee ($350/month minimum) plus required local marketing of at least $399/month in year one and 4% of gross receipts annually thereafter - on a median ~$203K revenue base, combined minimums are a meaningful fixed cost.
riskFlags.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and FDD Exchange)

riskFlags.title: Franchise fee is the bulk of the startup cost
riskFlags.detail: The $53,900 initial franchise fee accounts for roughly half to three-quarters of the $70,760-$117,150 total investment, leaving comparatively little budgeted working capital - underfunding the ramp is the common failure mode in low-cost service franchises.
riskFlags.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD (via FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)

nationwide.source: Franchising.com Caring Transitions profile; IFA franchise opportunity listing
nationwide.note: 325+ locations across the US with territories marketed nationwide; confirm specific territory availability with the franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD (via FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD (via FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)

franchiseFee.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross receipts; $300/month minimum in year one, $500/month thereafter.

adFundPercent.note: 2% national branding fee ($350/month minimum) plus local marketing of $399+/month year one and 4% of gross receipts annually thereafter.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross receipts across 253 full-year franchises; median $202,811; average gross profit 63%.

totalUnits.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)
totalUnits.note: All franchised; 0 company-owned. +58 net units in 2024.

veteranDiscount.note: 10% discount for qualifying military personnel and veterans.

isGrowing.source: Caring Transitions 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)

mobileBased.source: Franchise directory profiles (home-based senior relocation/estate sale services model)

businessModelType.source: Caring Transitions brand site (services sold to seniors and families)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Referral relationships with senior living communities, realtors, estate attorneys, and families plus online estate-sale platform (CTBids)
customerAcquisitionModel.source: Caring Transitions brand site / franchising.com profile

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2025 FDD); Franchise Chatter reviews

brands.name: First Choice Business Brokers
brands.notes: Las Vegas-based business brokerage franchise (founded 1994, franchising since 2005): franchisees run office territories where agents list and sell small businesses, earning commissions. Low entry cost, home-office capable, currently in a rapid expansion wave (+68% units per Entrepreneur). Income is 100% commission-based deal flow - no recurring revenue - and the FDD shows no Item 19 earnings claim per public summaries.

riskFlags.title: Double-digit royalty on commission revenue with no published earnings claim
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 10% of gross sales up to $850K (stepping down to 8%, then 7%) plus a 2% marketing fee and a $350+/month technology fee, while public FDD summaries show no Item 19 financial performance representation - buyers must validate broker commission economics entirely via franchisee calls.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter FCBB fee analysis; Vetted Biz (no lawsuits/Item 19 disclosed); Entrepreneur directory

riskFlags.title: Hyper-growth recruiting phase: +68% unit growth in a year
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur shows units jumping to 128 (+68.4%); directories counted ~109 a year earlier and 'over 95 territories sold across 34 states' in franchisor marketing - many offices are brand new, so system-wide support quality and validation cohort are thin.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; franchisor marketing via Business View Magazine

riskFlags.title: Mixed online franchisee/client reviews including fraud complaints
riskFlags.detail: Third-party review aggregations (IRAEmpire analysis, ComplaintsBoard) include client complaints alleging misleading listing practices at individual offices; these are office-level service complaints rather than franchisor litigation, but they signal reputation variance across territories.
riskFlags.source: IRAEmpire.com reviews analysis (via Fox5 San Diego press release); ComplaintsBoard

nationwide.note: Seeking franchisees throughout the US with exclusive territories; franchisor cites territories sold across 34 states.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Vetted Biz cites $69,150-$98,100 from the prior FDD.

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Chatter FCBB fee analysis; Entrepreneur directory
royaltyPercent.note: Tiered: 10% of gross sales to $850K, 8% on the next $850K, 7% thereafter.

adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing fee; plus $350+/month technology fee per Entrepreneur.

totalUnits.note: Vetted Biz counted 109 US locations in the prior year.

yearFounded.source: Business View Magazine; Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz (two-week immersive program at Las Vegas HQ); Entrepreneur lists 161 combined training hours

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing relationships available per Entrepreneur directory

businessModelType.source: FCBB brand site (brokerage services to business sellers and buyers)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Commission-based listings sourced through local agent networks, referrals, and business-for-sale marketplaces

fddAvailable.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS / FranchisePayback FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Vetted Biz / Franchise Chatter (no financial performance representation shown in public summaries)

brands.name: Curio Collection by Hilton
brands.notes: Hilton's upper-upscale 'soft brand' (launched 2014): independent, distinctive hotels keep their own identity while plugging into Hilton's reservation system and Honors program, franchised through Hilton Franchise Holding LLC. Institutional-scale hotel investment - mostly conversions of existing upscale properties - not a small-business franchise. Hilton does not publish a state-by-state availability list (development is negotiated market by market), so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Texas AG consumer-protection lawsuit against parent Hilton (pricing transparency)
riskFlags.detail: In 2023 the Texas Attorney General sued Hilton for violations of Texas consumer protection laws over hotel fee/price transparency (drip pricing of mandatory fees), part of a multi-state push affecting major hotel franchisors whose brand-level pricing practices flow down to franchised hotels.
riskFlags.source: Texas Attorney General press release

riskFlags.title: Extreme investment variability: $4.7M to $143M+ per the 2025 FDD
riskFlags.detail: Because Curio is a soft-brand of one-of-a-kind conversions and new builds, the 2025 FDD Item 7 for a typical 250-room hotel spans $4,683,359 to $143,454,204 (excluding real property), including up to ~$501,375 paid to the franchisor - underwriting depends entirely on the specific asset.
riskFlags.source: 2025 US Curio Franchise Disclosure Document (Hilton Franchise Holding LLC, public copy)

riskFlags.title: 5% royalty plus 4% program fee on Gross Rooms Revenue under a term that can run to 23 years
riskFlags.detail: Ongoing brand cost is a 5% royalty plus a 4% monthly program fee of Gross Rooms Revenue, with agreement terms of roughly 10-23 years - a long commitment to Hilton's system standards, PIP requirements, and fee schedule.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; 2025 US Curio FDD

riskFlags.title: Soft-brand identity cuts both ways
riskFlags.detail: Hotels keep independent names and positioning, so franchisees get Hilton distribution without full brand recognition; the value of the affiliation rests on Hilton Honors and channel economics rather than roadside brand awareness, and individual property reputations vary widely across the collection.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (soft-brand description); Hilton brand materials

totalInvestmentMin.source: 2025 US Curio FDD Item 7 (typical 250-room hotel, excluding real property)

franchiseFee.note: Earlier directory summaries cite $75,000; hotel application fees scale with room count.

royaltyPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; 2025 US Curio FDD

adFundPercent.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; 2025 US Curio FDD
adFundPercent.note: 4% monthly program fee of Gross Rooms Revenue.

totalUnits.note: Global collection count; roughly 45+ US per directory summaries.

renewalTermYears.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (term of agreement 10-23 years)
renewalTermYears.note: Hotel franchise terms are typically non-renewable as of right; relicensing negotiated.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing relationships available per Entrepreneur directory

isGrowing.note: +44.5% unit trend as Hilton scales the soft-brand portfolio.

internationalAvailability.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (US and international development)

brands.name: Eggs Up Grill
brands.notes: Southern breakfast/brunch/lunch franchise (founded 1997 in Pawleys Island, SC; franchising since 2008; owned by PE firm WJ Partners since 2018) with 110+ open restaurants and 170+ open or in development across the Southeast/Sun Belt. Single-shift 6am-2pm daypart model with 21 consecutive quarters of positive same-store sales. Franchisor publishes an available-territories page rather than an all-states statement.

riskFlags.title: Headline AUV is the top-50% figure, not the system average
riskFlags.detail: Franchisor marketing leads with $1.6M+ AUV and 19.7% EBITDA for the TOP 50% of restaurants; the systemwide 2024 AUV was about $1.347M and FDD-derived sources cite ~$1.293M - underwrite to the median, not the marketing number.
riskFlags.source: Eggs Up Grill franchise site (Item 19-based marketing); SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Small, regionally concentrated system scaling under PE ownership
riskFlags.detail: Only ~88 restaurants in the 2025 FDD outlet count (87 franchised, 1 company-owned), concentrated in the Southeast; WJ Partners has set aggressive growth targets (200+ units), and 60%+ of the system is multi-unit operators building out commitments - growth execution risk is real for new markets like Atlanta and Texas.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive franchise spotlight; Vetted Biz / FranchisePayback outlet data

riskFlags.title: Build cost has climbed to ~$822K-$1.14M
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD Item 7 range of $822,000-$1,141,000 is up substantially from earlier years' ~$500K-$700K ranges, pressuring returns at the ~$1.3M system AUV; the single-daypart model also caps revenue ceiling versus all-day concepts.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Eggs Up Grill FDD analysis (2025)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Eggs Up Grill 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

totalUnits.note: 87 franchised + 1 company-owned; franchisor cites 110+ open and 170+ open or in development in 2026.

brands.name: Bruster's Real Ice Cream
brands.notes: Bridgewater, PA scratch-made ice cream franchise (founded 1989, franchising since 1993): ~220 walk-up/drive-up stands that make ice cream fresh in-store daily from a proprietary home-style mix. Growing again (+11%) after years as a steady regional system. Seasonal walk-up format and a build cost that can approach $2.6M for ground-up sites are the key underwriting questions. Franchisor publishes target 'coming soon' states rather than an all-states statement.

riskFlags.title: Investment range spans $409K to $2.64M depending on real estate path
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD Item 7 runs $409,033-$2,644,060 - leased end-caps sit at the low end while ground-up freestanding stands with land acquisition can exceed $2.5M; at a ~$542K-$673K AUV, the heavy-build path has materially longer payback.
riskFlags.source: Bruster's 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Entrepreneur)

riskFlags.title: Seasonal, weather-dependent walk-up format
riskFlags.detail: The classic Bruster's is an outdoor walk-up/drive-up stand without indoor seating; sales concentrate in warm months and Sun Belt markets, and many northern stores reduce hours or close in winter - cash-flow planning must absorb the seasonal trough.
riskFlags.source: Bruster's brand fact sheet / franchise opportunity materials

riskFlags.title: Modest AUV versus QSR peers; verify Item 19 cohort definitions
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived sources cite a system AUV of $541,723 for stores open a full year, while directory estimates run ~$673,000 - the spread reflects different cohorts (all stores vs full-year stores), so confirm which Item 19 table applies to your market and format.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Bruster's FDD analysis; FranchisePayback

statesOpen.note: ~220 stores across roughly 22 states, concentrated in the East/Southeast. New 'coming soon' development targeted in AR, CO, ID, LA, MS, NE, NM, OR, WA; resales periodically available in AZ, GA, KY, NH, NC, RI, SC, TN. Confirm current territory availability with the franchisor.

franchiseFee.note: Some directories cite $35,000; veterans get 50% off.

avgUnitRevenue.note: System AUV for stores open a full year; directory estimates run ~$673,000.

yearFounded.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Bruster's brand history

yearFranchising.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Bruster's brand history

trainingWeeks.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (81.5 combined training hours)

veteranDiscount.note: 50% off the franchise fee (~$17,500-$20,000 savings) for military veterans.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing relationships available per Entrepreneur directory

seasonalityNotes.value: Outdoor walk-up format concentrates sales in warm months; northern stores commonly cut hours or close seasonally

brands.name: Port of Subs
brands.notes: Reno, NV fresh-sliced submarine sandwich brand (founded 1972, franchising since 1985), ~125-132 stores concentrated in seven western states. Acquired in April 2023 by Area 15 Ventures (RE/MAX co-founder Dave Liniger's investment firm), which is pushing national expansion through a regional-developer model. The franchisor does not publish an all-states availability list; expansion is being sold region by region.

riskFlags.title: Combined ongoing fees total ~10% of gross sales
riskFlags.detail: Franchisees pay a 6% royalty plus 1% advertising fund plus a required 3% local marketing spend - roughly 10 points of gross sales before rent and labor, on a system whose FDD-derived AUV is only ~$512K.
riskFlags.source: Port of Subs franchising FAQ (franchisor); Vetted Biz FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: New PE-style owner pushing rapid national expansion of a 7-state regional brand
riskFlags.detail: Area 15 Ventures acquired the 132-store, seven-state system in April 2023 and announced plans to award up to 50 development regions within twelve months - growth into markets with zero brand awareness is execution risk for both regional developers and unit franchisees.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive; IFA press release; franchisor announcements

riskFlags.title: Modest AUV against a $420K-$857K build
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived yearly gross sales of $512,334 with estimated owner earnings of ~$61K-$77K imply a long payback at the midpoint of the $419,895-$856,875 investment range - favor lower-cost non-traditional or end-cap sites.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Port of Subs Franchise Insights; SHARPSHEETS

totalInvestmentMin.source: Port of Subs franchising FAQ / investment page (franchisor)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Port of Subs franchising FAQ / investment page (franchisor)

adFundPercent.note: Plus required 3% of gross sales local marketing spend.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Port of Subs FDD-derived analysis (Vetted Biz)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales; estimated owner earnings $61,481-$76,851 per Vetted Biz.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz - Port of Subs Franchise Insights
totalUnits.note: 132 stores in 7 western states at the April 2023 acquisition; franchisor cites 125+ locations.

yearFounded.source: IFA / franchisor brand history (first shop in Sparks, NV)

trainingWeeks.note: 30-50 hours self-study plus ~3 weeks in-store training (Reno, Las Vegas, or Phoenix) plus up to 1 week at Reno HQ; two Certified Managers required per location.

businessModelType.note: Plus meaningful B2B catering/box-lunch business in many stores.

mobileBased.source: Port of Subs brand site (storefront QSR model)

isGrowing.source: Franchisor expansion announcements under Area 15 Ventures (regional developer sales)
isGrowing.note: Unit count has been roughly flat (~126-132); growth claim rests on the post-2023 regional-developer pipeline rather than realized openings.

brands.name: Carl's Jr.
brands.notes: Charbroiled-burger QSR chain founded 1941 (Carl Karcher's hot dog cart), franchised by CKE Restaurants (also parent of Hardee's; owned by Roark Capital). ~1,120 US locations, 96% franchised, concentrated in California and the West. The system is in slight decline, and 2026 brought a high-profile 65-unit California franchisee bankruptcy that blamed the $20 fast-food minimum wage and lack of brand support. CKE develops via multi-unit agreements market by market; no all-states availability list is published.

riskFlags.title: 65-unit California franchisee filed Chapter 11 (2026), closing stores and blaming the brand
riskFlags.detail: Friendly Franchisees Corp., a major Southern California Carl's Jr. operator with 65 locations, filed Chapter 11 in April 2026, planning ~10 closures and the sale of 49 restaurants; the owner publicly blamed California's $20 fast-food minimum wage and 'lack of support and innovation' from Carl's Jr.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive; QSR Magazine; Fast Company

riskFlags.title: Declining units and soft AUV trend
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived data shows ~1,020 franchised US units in 2024 with a roughly -1% annual decline, and Circana's 2026 restaurant ranking pegs Carl's Jr. AUV at ~$1.4M, down 2.7% - a mature brand losing ground in the burger segment.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback FDD data; Circana Definitive U.S. Restaurant Ranking 2026 (via trade coverage)

riskFlags.title: California concentration exposes operators to the state's wage and regulatory regime
riskFlags.detail: The brand's home market and densest footprint is California, where the AB 1228 $20+ fast-food minimum wage and FAST-Act-style regulation compress margins - the exact pressure cited in the 2026 franchisee bankruptcy.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive / KTLA coverage of franchisee closures

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 AUV disclosure plus 10% combined royalty and ad burden
riskFlags.detail: CKE's Carl's Jr. FDD does not disclose average unit volumes (external estimates ~$1.4-1.57M), while ongoing fees run 4% royalty plus ~6% advertising - among the heaviest marketing loads in QSR, on top of a $1.3M-$3.4M build.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback (no AUV in FDD); SHARPSHEETS fee analysis; Carl's Jr. 2023 FDD (public copy, restfinance.com)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Carl's Jr. 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Carl's Jr. 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

franchiseFee.source: Carl's Jr. FDD (via FranchisePayback; directories cite $25,000-$35,000 by units developed)

royaltyPercent.source: Carl's Jr. FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

adFundPercent.source: Carl's Jr. FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Circana Definitive U.S. Restaurant Ranking 2026 (via trade coverage)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Not disclosed in the FDD; FranchisePayback's external estimate is $1,568,000.

multiUnitAvailable.source: CKE franchising site (multi-unit development agreements standard)

isGrowing.source: FranchisePayback FDD-derived unit data (-1% YoY); Circana AUV trend (-2.7%)

mobileBased.source: Carl's Jr. brand site (drive-thru QSR model)

internationalAvailability.source: CKE Restaurants international franchising (Carl's Jr. operates in 25+ countries)

fddAvailable.source: Public Carl's Jr. FDD copy (restfinance.com, 2023); SHARPSHEETS 2025 analysis

item19Available.source: FranchisePayback (franchisor does not disclose AUV in FDD)

brands.name: Wetzel's Pretzels
brands.notes: Fresh-baked soft pretzel franchise founded 1994 in Pasadena, CA (franchising since 1996), now owned by Canadian multi-brand platform MTY Food Group. ~425 units (389 franchised) growing steadily, with a strong published Item 19 (17.4% net operating income at benchmark mall stores). Core format is regional/outlet mall counters; newer Walmart, transit, and food-truck formats earn roughly half the mall AUV. No all-states availability statement published; franchisor qualifies candidates market by market.

riskFlags.title: Format choice swings revenue by 2-3x
riskFlags.detail: FY2024 Item 19 average adjusted gross revenue: entertainment centers $1,004,656 and outlet malls $998,259 versus Walmart locations $566,694, non-traditional/transit $530,472, and concession trucks just $281,094 - the same fees and brand on radically different revenue bases.
riskFlags.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Mall-traffic dependence in the core format
riskFlags.detail: The highest-earning formats are regional and outlet mall bakeries (192 of 269 reporting franchises), tying unit economics to mall foot traffic and mall rent (17.8% of net sales at benchmark stores) at a time of secular mall-traffic pressure.
riskFlags.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD Item 19 benchmark P&L (via Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: Brand is one of 90+ in the MTY Food Group portfolio
riskFlags.detail: MTY Food Group acquired Wetzel's Pretzels in late 2022 (~$207M); franchisees are now part of a large multi-brand franchisor platform whose capital allocation, supply programs, and support resources are shared across dozens of concepts.
riskFlags.source: MTY Food Group / mtyfranchising.com; Franchise Chatter

riskFlags.title: Streetside locations carry a different fee split (5% royalty + 3% ad fund)
riskFlags.detail: Standard bakeries pay 7% royalty + 1% ad fund, while 'streetside' bakeries pay 5% + 3%; the franchisor may also waive or cut the $40K fee for non-traditional sites at its sole discretion - compare the exact schedule for your format.
riskFlags.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter fee analysis)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS cites a wider $62,000-$712,000 range including low-cost non-traditional formats.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

franchiseFee.note: May be waived/reduced for non-traditional locations (convenience stores, travel plazas, Walmart) at franchisor discretion.

royaltyPercent.note: 7% of adjusted gross revenue; 5% for streetside bakeries.

adFundPercent.note: 1% of adjusted gross revenue; 3% for streetside bakeries.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: FY2024 average adjusted gross revenue across 269 franchised bakeries; median $727,125. 2023 benchmark mall stores: $952,067 net sales with 17.4% net operating income.

totalUnits.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter/Vetted Biz)

yearFounded.source: Wetzel's Pretzels brand history (Pasadena, CA); franchise directory profiles

isGrowing.source: Wetzel's Pretzels 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)
isGrowing.note: Franchised units +12 (2022), +25 (2023), +42 (2024); expansion into Walmart and food trucks.

mobileBased.source: Wetzel's brand site (counter/kiosk model; small concession-truck program exists)

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter / Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS 2025 FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2026 review (detailed Item 19 by format with benchmark P&L)

brands.name: Zoomin Groomin
brands.notes: Mobile pet-grooming franchise (founded 2004, franchising since 2009; acquired 2020 by John Hewitt's Loyalty Brands umbrella). Van-based, home-based-office model that exploded from 15 to 200+ vans/territories across ~32 states since 2022. Item 19 is built on a tiny mature cohort (11 full-year vehicles averaging ~$206K), so most of the system has no track record yet - plus groomer hiring is the operational bottleneck.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 based on only 11 full-year vehicles in a system of 70+
riskFlags.detail: The 2024 FDD's earnings claim covers just 11 vehicles operating a full year (average sales $206,495; median $202,875; range $122,459-$271,881) while 55 newly sold 2023 units were excluded - the published numbers describe a small early cohort, not the rapidly recruited majority.
riskFlags.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Territory metrics changed: new buyers get smaller territories than existing franchisees
riskFlags.detail: The FDD discloses that Zoomin Groomin changed how it sizes territories and that existing franchisees hold larger territories than the one now being offered - revenue benchmarks from older, larger territories may overstate what a new smaller territory can produce.
riskFlags.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Hyper-growth recruiting: 15 to 200+ units in about three years
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units went 4 (2021) to 15 (2022) to 70 (2023) and ~211 by 2025 - recruiting far outpaces the mature validation base, and system support, groomer recruiting pipelines, and van supply must scale in real time.
riskFlags.source: FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter); franchising.com sponsored profile (200+ vans, 32 states)

riskFlags.title: 8% royalty with weekly minimums on a ~$200K revenue base
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 8% of gross revenues paid weekly (minimum $125/week) plus a 2% brand fund - a high percentage for a labor-constrained, single-van service business where the certified groomer is the revenue engine.
riskFlags.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

statesOpen.note: 200+ vans across 32 states as of 2025; franchisor markets remaining territories broadly. Confirm specific territory availability with the franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter)
totalInvestmentMin.note: SHARPSHEETS cites $96,000-$188,000; Franchise Direct cites $64,974-$205,400 from a different FDD year.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter)

franchiseFee.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and Vetted Biz)

avgUnitRevenue.source: Zoomin Groomin 2024 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2023 average annual sales per vehicle across only 11 full-year vehicles; median $202,875.

totalUnits.source: Franchising.com growth profile (2025); FDD outlet tables showed 70 franchised + 4 company-owned at end of 2023

isGrowing.source: FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter); franchising.com 2025 profile

mobileBased.source: Zoomin Groomin brand site (van-based mobile grooming)

businessModelType.source: Zoomin Groomin brand site (residential pet owners)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Local digital marketing and recurring-appointment subscriptions; route density built around repeat residential grooming customers
customerAcquisitionModel.source: Zoomin Groomin brand site / FranchiseWire profile

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter / Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2024 review (Item 19 disclosed, small cohort)

brands.name: Mister Sparky
brands.notes: 'America's On-Time Electrician' - residential electrical service franchise in the Authority Brands portfolio, ~215 locations (209 franchised) and growing fast (+45 units in 2024). Strong category tailwinds and a real Item 19, but the average is carried by huge multi-territory operators: median territory revenue is less than half the mean. Conversion of an existing electrical business is the cheapest path in. No published all-states availability list; territories sold by population.

riskFlags.title: Mean/median gap: median territory does $581K vs $1.28M average
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19 (50 franchisees, 146 territories): average gross revenue per territory was $1,284,907 but the median was just $580,809; top-quartile territories averaged $3,537,392 while the bottom quartile averaged $194,119 - outcomes are heavily skewed toward large established operators.
riskFlags.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Royalty minimums plus heavy recommended local marketing spend
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 6% of gross revenue or $1,500/month, whichever is HIGHER - a fixed floor during ramp-up - and the franchisor recommends spending 8%-12% of gross revenue on local marketing on top of the tiered brand fund (1.5% on the first $5M).
riskFlags.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: One of many brands in PE-backed Authority Brands; licensed-trade labor is the bottleneck
riskFlags.detail: Mister Sparky sits in the Authority Brands multi-brand home-services platform (The Cleaning Authority, Benjamin Franklin Plumbing, One Hour Heating & Air, etc.); franchisee growth depends on recruiting licensed electricians in a national skilled-trades shortage, and master-electrician licensing requirements vary by state.
riskFlags.source: Authority Brands portfolio; 1851 Franchise Mister Sparky profile

totalInvestmentMin.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter and SHARPSHEETS)
totalInvestmentMin.note: New startup. Conversions of existing electrical businesses: ~$84,570-$204,824.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter and SHARPSHEETS)

franchiseFee.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and PeerSense)
franchiseFee.note: Plus $0.165 per person above 200,000 territory population.

royaltyPercent.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and Franchise Chatter)
royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross revenue or $1,500/month, whichever is greater.

adFundPercent.note: Tiered brand fund: 1.5% on first $5M of revenue, stepping down to 0% above $20M; 8%-12% local marketing recommended.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross revenue per territory (146 territories); median $580,809.

totalUnits.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD outlet tables (via SHARPSHEETS/Franchise Chatter)

isGrowing.source: Mister Sparky FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)
isGrowing.note: Franchised units +23 (2022), +25 (2023), +45 (2024); system revenue +6.7% YoY across continuing territories.

businessModelType.source: Mister Sparky brand site (residential electrical service)

mobileBased.source: Mister Sparky brand site (van-based service business; small shop/office)

customerAcquisitionModel.value: Local digital marketing/SEO and brand-fund-driven lead generation with on-time service guarantee positioning; recommended 8-12% of revenue local marketing spend
customerAcquisitionModel.source: Mister Sparky 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS); franchisor marketing

fddAvailable.source: SHARPSHEETS / Franchise Chatter / Vetted Biz 2025 FDD analyses

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2026 review (Item 19 with quartiles disclosed)

brands.name: Image360
brands.notes: B2B signs, graphics, and visual-communications franchise in Alliance Franchise Brands' Sign & Graphics Division (which also includes Signs By Tomorrow and Signs Now; lineage dates to 1986, with the Image360 brand launched in 2013 as the rebrand/consolidation flag). ~125-133 US Image360 centers across 34 states. Solid B2B repeat-customer economics ($675K gross, ~4-6 year payback per Vetted Biz), but the combined sign-division network has been shrinking as legacy-brand owners retire or exit.

riskFlags.title: Shrinking network (-7% unit trend)
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's directory shows 272 units with a -7% trend (count includes affiliated sign brands being consolidated under Image360); FDD-derived counts show ~125 franchised Image360 centers - a contracting legacy network rather than a growth system, which affects resale liquidity and peer support.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz / FranchiseGrade FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Royalty schedule is variable (1.5%-6% with caps) - model your actual rate
riskFlags.detail: Public sources disagree on the royalty (Entrepreneur: 1.5%-6%; FranchiseGrade: '6%/2% with caps'; Vetted Biz: 4%), reflecting a tiered/capped schedule that varies with revenue and legacy agreements - get the exact royalty table from the current FDD before modeling returns.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur directory; FranchiseGrade; Vetted Biz (conflicting royalty disclosures)

riskFlags.title: Lawsuits/bankruptcy information disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz notes Image360's FDD discloses litigation and/or bankruptcy information (Items 3-4); review the specific disclosures with counsel - multi-brand franchisors with decades of history typically carry some case history.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Image360 Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Owner-operator B2B sales role with staffed storefront
riskFlags.detail: No absentee, home-based, or part-time operation is permitted; the model expects an owner actively selling to local businesses from a staffed production storefront - hiring production/design staff and building a commercial client book are the ramp risks.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

nationwide.note: Seeking franchisees throughout the US; existing centers in 34 states. Conversions of independent sign shops also marketed.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Image360 FDD Item 7 (via Vetted Biz and Franchise Gator)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $139,137 low end (likely conversion of an existing sign business).

totalInvestmentMax.source: Image360 FDD Item 7 (via Vetted Biz and Entrepreneur)

franchiseFee.source: Image360 FDD (via Vetted Biz and Entrepreneur; disclosed $40,000-$45,000)

royaltyPercent.note: Sources conflict (Vetted Biz cites 4%); tiered/capped schedule - confirm in current FDD.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales; Vetted Biz estimates owner-operator earnings of $101,192-$134,923 and a 4-6 year payback.

totalUnits.note: Franchised Image360 centers in 34 states; Vetted Biz counts 133; Entrepreneur's 272 includes affiliated Alliance sign brands.

yearFounded.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (predecessor Signs By Tomorrow lineage)
yearFounded.note: Image360 brand itself launched 2013 as the consolidation of Signs By Tomorrow/Signs Now centers.

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz (10-day HQ program); Entrepreneur lists 166 combined hours

mobileBased.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (storefront/production center; home-based not permitted)

businessModelType.source: Vetted Biz / Image360 brand site (custom signage, displays, vehicle wraps for businesses)

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing relationships available per Entrepreneur directory

isGrowing.note: -7% unit trend across the consolidated sign division.

fddAvailable.source: Free FDD Library (Image360/Signs By FDD); Vetted Biz analysis
